title: Issues with Monday.com SDK API Error Handling

posts.author: tryve-frederik (Frederik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

The monday.com Apps framework SDK for JavaScript (last updated 6 months ago) appears to be outdated compared to the newer Monday GraphQL JS SDK (updated 22 days ago).

There are some issues with how the ‘old’ SDK handles errors from the API. These issues are resolved in the new SDK, but this SDK requires an access token, meaning that we would have to implement OAuth to be able to us this. While the old sdk does not require this.

  • The sdk only the error message, omitting the full error with errors array:

    1. Limited error information: Only the error message is returned
    2. References issues:
      • How to get the full api error list using monday js sdk? - #5 by codyfrisch
      • [Bug] Error handling is not correct · Issue #145 · mondaycom/monday-sdk-js · GitHub

  • The sdk uses window.postMessage for API communication:

    1. sdk sends request info to monday.com via postMessage
    2. monday.com makes the actual API call
    3. If an error occurs, monday.com throws it, appearing in the browser console

  • This approach leads to two issues:

    1. Redundant error handling: We handle errors in our app, while monday.com separately logs them
    2. Limited control: We can’t suppress or manage these console errors from our end

  • The sdk handles errors inconsistently for server-side and client-side operations:

    1. Server-side errors: Received in the response as expected
    2. Client-side errors: An error is thrown instead of being included in the response

posts.author: idan-dover (Idan Dover)
posts.cookedHtml: I also found this to be limiting in an dashboard widget I am building. Any news about how to get the full error on a front end app?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @idan-dover,

If you would be able to provide more context on what your scenario is and what you would like to see exactly, that would be really helpful for our team following this feature request!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: idan-dover (Idan Dover)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Matias.Monday,

Thank you for the time investing in the subject. In the widget I build, I pass all the relevant item IDs for the dashboard and use the Monday SDK to fetch the necessary data. However, when handling a large number of items, I occasionally encounter rate limit or complexity errors. While this is understandable, the SDK only provides generic error messages like “Rate limit passed” or “GraphQL validations error.”

On the server side, the API’s error handling is excellent, as it provides specific details, including the time remaining until requests can be resumed. Unfortunately, this information isn’t available in the SDK on the client. As a result, I have to implement a workaround to avoid hitting the rate limit. This involves making requests at a slower pace and, in the event of hitting the limit, defaulting to a conservative wait time (approximately one minute) before retrying.

To address this, I’ve resorted to using a server as a proxy to relay the requests. While functional, this approach adds an unnecessary layer that simply forwards the same requests from the client to your API and also gives worse user experience because of the added time to receive the data.

It would be highly beneficial if the SDK could provide more detailed error information on the client, similar to what is available on the server side. This enhancement would allow for better handling of rate limits without relying on a server proxy.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the context @idan-dover !
